{{Quote|You're bluffing, Jabot. But I'll show you who's the better Helcos player. I'll match your wager and I'll triple it.|The Borlorian Merex|<ref name="Galladinium" />}}
The '''Borlorians''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]]<ref name="Galladinium" /> originating from<ref name="Planetnamia" /> the [[Borloria system/Legends|Borloria system]] in the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region's]] [[Dentari sector/Legends|Dentari sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> Members of the species were able to sneer. During the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], [[Merex]] was a [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]]–speaking Borlorian who had a [[Standard year/Legends|years]]-long rivalry with the [[Alien/Legends|alien]]<ref name="Galladinium" /> [[Gambler/Legends|gambler]] and [[Con artist/Legends|con artist]]<ref name="EGWT">''[[The Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]''</ref> [[Jabot]]. At one point, the two were the last two remaining players at a game of [[Helcos]]. Jabot—who was communicating via an [[implant communicator]] with his partner [[Dereth]], who fed him information on Merex's cards—manipulated Merex into matching his bet of ten thousand [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] and then tripling the wager. After Jabot ultimately revealed his superior hand of cards, the Borlorian howled in discontent, resulting in [[Glass/Legends|glasses]] around the room they were in shattering.<ref name="Galladinium" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Borlorians were introduced in ''[[Galladinium's Fantastic Technology]]'', a [[1995]] sourcebook authored by [[Rick D. Stuart]] for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Galladinium" />
